channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A long-lost ancient Roman artifact reappears in a New Orleans backyard
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A New Orleans family cleaning up their overgrown backyard made an extremely unusual find: Under the weeds was a mysterious marble tablet with Latin characters that included the phrase “spirits of the dead.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: There are no survivors in the blast at a Tennessee explosives factory, sheriff says
item.description: McEWEN, Tenn. (AP) — The blast in rural Tennessee that leveled an explosives plant and was felt for miles around left no survivors, authorities said Saturday. The total number of dead was unclear, as was the cause of the Friday blast.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: As the National Guard enters Memphis, memories of MLK and 1968 unrest resurface
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — As National Guard troops arrive in Memphis, the memory of thousands of them with bayonetted rifles and tanks in 1968 is still fresh for Joe Calhoun. Back then, he marched in the streets with sanitation workers and the Rev. Martin Luther King Jr.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE and ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pro-Palestinian protesters march to stadium ahead of World Cup qualifier between Norway and Israel
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — Pro-Palestinian protesters lit flares and waved flags as they marched to the stadium ahead of the World Cup qualifier between Norway and Israel on Saturday. Public broadcaster NRK said around 1,000 protesters arrived at the Ullevaal Stadion in Oslo.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ivory Coast arrests 237 protesters amid rising tensions before presidential election
item.description: ABIDJAN, Ivory Coast (AP) — At least 237 people were arrested Saturday in Ivory Coast during a protest against what activists called the country's authoritarian drift, according to a statement by the Minister of the Interior and Security on national television.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: France's re-appointed prime minister Lecornu calls for calm amid political chaos
item.description: L'HAY-LES-ROSES, France (AP) — France’s newly re-appointed prime minister acknowledged Saturday that there weren’t ″a lot of candidates″ for his job — and that he might not last long in the post given the country’s deep political divides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyers seek dismissal of federal charges in assassination of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: **Video is previous coverage of Luigi Mangione pleading not guilty to charges in December 2024** NEW YORK (AP) — Lawyers for Luigi Mangione asked a New York federal judge Saturday to dismiss some criminal charges, including the only count for which he could face the death penalty, from a federal ind
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, KRON4

item.title: Biden is receiving radiation and hormone therapy to treat his prostate cancer
item.description: Former President Joe Biden's treatment for an aggressive form of prostate cancer has entered a new phase.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 7 risk factors of deadly fungus infections identified, new Candida auris research finds
item.description: Researchers are getting a better idea of what leads to clinical cases of Candida auris, a dangerous and drug-resistant fungus that has been rapidly multiplying.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A sacred lake, a Columbus statue, and an Indigenous people's long struggle for land
item.description: GEDDES, N.Y. (AP) — Before white settlers came to Onondaga Lake, before the city of Syracuse grew along its shore, before the pristine waters became one of the most polluted bodies of water in the United States, it was a sacred place for the Onondaga Nation.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Passenger says Uber driver accused in Palisades Fire was ‘nut job’ who ranted about Trump
item.description: Passenger Brennan White describes Palisades Fire suspect Jonathan Rinderknecht as a "nut job" who bashed President Donald Trump during Uber ride.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blast at a Tennessee explosives plant leaves 18 missing and feared dead, sheriff says
item.description: McEWEN, Tenn. (AP) — A blast leveled an explosives plant Friday in rural Tennessee, leaving behind a mass of twisted metal, burned-out shells of cars and at least 18 people missing and feared dead, authorities said. “There’s nothing to describe.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OSHA violations reported prior to massive explosion at Tennessee facility: documents
item.description: As investigators continue to comb through the wreckage of an explosion in Tennessee that left 18 people unaccounted for Friday, Nexstar's WKRN has learned more about the company that operated the facility.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Wilson, Fox 5

item.title: OSHA violations reported prior to massive explosion at Tennessee facility: documents
item.description: As investigators continue to comb through the wreckage of an explosion in Tennessee that left 18 people unaccounted for Friday, Nexstar's WKRN has learned more about the company that operated the facility.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Wilson, KRON4

item.title: AstraZeneca agrees to lower drug prices for Medicaid under Trump administration deal
item.description: AstraZeneca on Friday became the second major pharmaceutical manufacturer to announce it had agreed to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Medicaid under a deal struck with the Trump administration that avoided its threats of steep tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, beloved chaplain at Loyola University Chicago, dies at 106
item.description: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, a beloved member of the Loyola University Chicago community who was well-known as the chaplain to the Loyola University men's basketball team, died Thursday at the age of 106, according to the university.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: Air traffic controllers who duck unpaid work during the gov't shutdown could be fired, Duffy warns
item.description: Even though the U.S. has a stark shortage of air traffic controllers, ones who call in sick instead of working without a paycheck during the federal government shutdown risk being fired, the U.S. transportation secretary warned.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Most states planning to withdraw National Guard troops from DC this fall
item.description: Nearly half the roughly 2,450 National Guard troops deployed to Washington, D.C., amid President Donald Trump's crackdown on crime are reportedly set to leave the city later this fall.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Gradeschoolers leave Las Vegas school campus without teachers noticing
item.description: "There was no footage, and nobody noticed that they were gone. My whole thing is how is a kid able to wonder off, and then the teacher doesn't notice that there's an empty seat in the class," Christian Peña said.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, NewsNation

item.title: Missing 23-year-old Philadelphia woman harassed before disappearing: Police
item.description: She told family and friends before she disappeared that she was being harassed by unidentified people over the phone, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Some Palestinians pack up and move north toward their homes in Gaza after ceasefire goes into effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians displaced by the war in Gaza started walking north Friday toward homes they were forced to flee — or what remains of them — after the Israeli military announced a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas had gone into effect.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Kilmar Abrego Garcia: Attorneys argue smuggling charges should be thrown out for 'vindictive' prosecution
item.description: Attorneys for Kilmar Abrego Garcia were in court Friday morning arguing the human smuggling charges against their client should be dismissed for vindictive prosecution.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Mayer, NewsNation

item.title: Travelers continue to endure delays amid government shutdown
item.description: On Thursday, Duffy told Fox Business that he may fire air traffic controllers who haven't shown up for work.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Judge orders suspect in deadly LA wildfire to remain jailed, citing mental health concerns
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — A federal judge in Florida ordered the man charged in California's deadly Palisades Fire to remain jailed Thursday after a prosecutor said he had traits of an arsonist and his family had worried about his declining mental state.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER and CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Turning Point USA announces 'All American' Super Bowl halftime show
item.description: Turning Point USA, the grassroots conservative political action network founded by the late activist Charlie Kirk, is planning a Super Bowl halftime show with the goal of competing with the official show featuring Bad Bunny and sponsored by Apple Music.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: ICE agents clash with cemetery workers attempting to help man in river
item.description: It is currently unknown whether the cemetery workers involved will face charges in connection with the incident on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Julian Crews, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gene Simmons admits to being a 'horrible driver' after fainting, crashing into parked car
item.description: KISS rocker Gene Simmons is thanking fans for their concern after he crashed his SUV earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WGN 9

item.title: New Jersey businessman who testified against ex-Sen. Bob Menendez won't go to prison
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New Jersey businessman who testified against former Democratic Sen. Bob Menendez and his wife at separate bribery trials won’t go to prison, after a judge credited him at his sentencing Thursday for showing honesty on the witness stand and sincere remorse.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Top DHS official defends ICE agents filmed shooting pepper ball at pastor's head
item.description: (The Hill) – Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Assistant Secretary Tricia McLaughlin on Wednesday defended an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officer who shot a pastor with a pepper ball from a rooftop after video of the incident went viral this week. Videos of the Sept.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: World leaders express hope after Trump says Israel and Hamas agreed to first phase of peace deal
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — World leaders on Thursday expressed hope for peace and urged Israel and Hamas to fulfill their commitments in the hours after U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nobel Prize in chemistry goes to discovery that could trap C02 and bring water to deserts
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Three scientists won the Nobel Prize in chemistry Wednesday for their development of new molecular structures that can trap vast quantities of gas inside, laying the groundwork to potentially suck greenhouse gases out of the atmosphere or harvest moisture from desert environments.
item.dc:creator: KOSTYA MANENKOV, STEFANIE DAZIO and CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: GOP senators increasingly anxious about Trump’s aggressive use of National Guard
item.description: Republican senators are increasingly uneasy about President Trump’s standoff with Democratic governors over deploying National Guard troops from other states to Portland, Ore., and Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, ABC 4

item.title: Authorities charge suspect, 29, with starting deadly Palisades Fire
item.description: Authorities arrested 29-year-old Jonathan Rinderknecht for lighting a fire on New Year’s Day that burned down much of the Pacific Palisades a week later.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Weber, WGN 9

item.title: Richard Allen's attorneys reveal strategy in appeal of Delphi murders conviction
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS — A motion filed this week in the appeal of Richard Allen's double murder conviction reveals at least two issues his appellate attorneys plan to challenge in their attempts to reverse his conviction and 120-year prison sentence.
item.dc:creator: Max Lewis, WGN 9

item.title: Robert Roberson is again approaching execution in Texas in shaken baby case
item.description: LIVINGSTON, Texas (AP) — Robert Roberson was calm and hopeful as he pondered his mortality and whether he could again avoid becoming the first person in the U.S. executed for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome. With days to go before his scheduled Oct.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: October Prime Day has massive generator deals
item.description: With a generator, you can juice up your devices when the battery runs low and use the flashlight in dark situations.
item.dc:creator: Jmar Gambol, Fox 5

item.title: White House says it will use tariff money to fund nutrition program amid shutdown
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt posted on X that President Trump and the White House had identified the "creative solution" to shift tariff funds to the Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants and Children, commonly known as WIC, which was set to run out of money in th
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
